myAssets.storageRuleFreeRetention: Free Users: Videos and images are auto-deleted 24 hours after creation.
myAssets.storageRulePro: Pro Users: Stored permanently while your subscription is active.
myAssets.storageRuleQuota: Universal 1GB storage cap. When storage is full, the oldest items are removed first.
myAssets.storageRuleSoftDelete: Deleted items stay in a 7-day recycle window before permanent removal.

guide.title: Video Agent Guide
guide.subtitle: Learn how to turn an idea into a fully produced video in minutes.
guide.introBody: Video Agent is VidFab's end-to-end AI workflow. Give it a story idea — typed from scratch or remixed from a YouTube Shorts clip — and it returns a finished video with characters, storyboards, motion, and music. No timeline editing required.
guide.twoWaysSubhead: Pick whichever fits — both end up in the same generation pipeline.
guide.referenceStep1Body: Use a public Shorts video under 1 minute. Non-dialogue clips with clear scenes work best.
guide.referenceStep2Body: Realistic, Anime, 3D Animation, Cinematic — your style sets the look of every character and shot.
guide.referenceStep3Body: Off = silent (default, saves credits). On = Seedance native audio (costs 2× credits).
guide.referenceStep4Body: AI extracts the story beats and drops you into the editor for script review.
guide.myselfStep1Title: Write your story (or hit AI Inspiration)
guide.myselfStep1Body: Type freely or click AI Inspiration for ready-made story seeds you can edit.
guide.myselfStep2Title: Tune duration, style, aspect ratio, music
guide.myselfStep2Body: Duration impacts shot count and credits. Aspect 9:16 fits Shorts, 16:9 fits landscape.
guide.myselfStep3Body: Your project enters the same automated pipeline below.
guide.behindSubhead: 4 automated stages run sequentially. You can review and regenerate any stage.
guide.stageScriptBody: AI breaks your story into shots, characters, and pacing. Edit anything before moving on.
guide.stageCharacterBody: Each character gets a consistent visual identity that carries through every shot.
guide.stageStoryboardBody: Per-shot still images with mood, camera angle, and characters in place. Regenerate any shot you don't like.
guide.stageCompositionBody: Each storyboard becomes a moving clip; clips are stitched into a final video with the music you chose.
guide.tip1: Drafts auto-save while you work. Open "Your Drafts" from the quota row to resume.
guide.tip2: Free plan: 5 scripts per month. After that, each script costs 3 credits.
guide.tip3: A full video typically takes 5–15 minutes to generate. You can leave the page; status persists.
guide.tip4: You can edit the script after analysis but before character generation starts.
guide.faq1Q: What kind of YouTube videos work best?
guide.faq1A: Non-dialogue Shorts under 1 minute, with clear scenes and recognizable characters.
guide.faq2Q: Can I change a character or shot after generation?
guide.faq2A: Yes — regenerate any character card or storyboard individually before the final video composition step.
guide.faq3Q: What happens to my draft if I close the browser?
guide.faq3A: Drafts auto-save server-side. Resume them anytime from the Drafts page.
guide.footerCtaTitle: Ready to make your first video?
